evals.prompt: What's the current price of AirPods Pro (2nd generation) on Amazon? Just give me the price and product title — nothing else.
evals.expected_output: A short response with the product title and current price extracted from Amazon, e.g. 'Apple AirPods Pro (2nd generation) — $189.99'

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract
assertions.description: The agent ran nimble extract with an Amazon URL

assertions.name: contains_price
assertions.description: The output contains a dollar amount (price)

assertions.name: contains_product_title
assertions.description: The output contains 'AirPods' in the product name

assertions.name: no_curl_or_requests
assertions.description: The agent did not use curl, wget, requests, or WebFetch to get the data

evals.prompt: What are the latest news stories about Anthropic from the past week? Give me a bullet list of 5 headlines with a one-sentence summary each.
evals.expected_output: A bullet list of 5 recent Anthropic news headlines with brief summaries, sourced from a live web search

assertions.name: uses_nimble_search
assertions.description: The agent ran nimble search with a query about Anthropic

assertions.name: uses_news_focus_or_time_range
assertions.description: The search used --focus news or --time-range week/day to get recent results

assertions.name: has_five_bullets
assertions.description: The output contains at least 5 bullet points or numbered items

evals.prompt: I need to understand what Python's itertools module offers. Can you go to docs.python.org and find the itertools page, then give me a summary of its main function groups?
evals.expected_output: A structured summary of Python itertools grouped by type (e.g. infinite iterators, combinatoric iterators, terminating iterators), extracted from the live Python docs

assertions.name: uses_nimble_extract_or_map
assertions.description: The agent ran nimble extract or nimble map targeting docs.python.org

assertions.name: covers_main_groups
assertions.description: The output mentions at least 2 of: infinite iterators, combinatoric iterators, chain, islice, groupby, product, combinations, permutations

assertions.name: structured_output
assertions.description: The output is organized into categories or groups, not just a flat list

assertions.name: no_hallucination_check
assertions.description: The output doesn't describe functions that don't exist in itertools (e.g. itertools.map, itertools.filter)
